explanation_md: There are no `break` statements, so after matching `case 2:` and printing `two`, execution falls through into `case 3:` (printing `three`) and then into `default:` (printing `other`). Fallthrough continues until the end of the switch body or a `break` is reached.

prompt_md: Which of the following types is **not** valid as the expression inside `switch (...)`?
explanation_md: `switch` requires an integral type â€” `int`, `char`, `bool`, and similar types are all fine because they are integers underneath. `double` is a floating-point type and the compiler rejects it as a switch expression.

`month` is `9` and `case 9:` is one of the stacked labels that shares the `"30 days"` block. The stacked labels are not fallthrough â€” they all point to the same block, so only `30 days` is printed before `break` exits the switch.

prompt_md:
A `case` label value must be:

A â€” any expression that evaluates at runtime
B â€” a compile-time integer constant
C â€” a string literal
D â€” a variable declared earlier in the same function

options.a: Any expression that evaluates at runtime
options.d: A variable declared earlier in the same function

explanation_md: Case labels must be compile-time integer constants so the compiler can build the jump table. Variables (even `const` ones initialized at runtime) and string literals are not allowed. Using a variable as a case label is a compile error.

options.a: The `default` label must come before the numbered cases
options.b: `case 0:` is missing a `break`, so execution falls through and also prints `Boiling`
options.c: `int` is not a valid switch expression type
options.d: `case 100:` should be `case 1:` to match boolean logic

explanation_md: After printing `Freezing`, there is no `break`, so execution falls into `case 100:` and prints `Boiling` as well. Adding `break;` after `std::cout << "Freezing\n";` fixes it.
